title: Smoky Hot Canadian Fish Fritters
description: A pescatarian-friendly dish featuring smoky, glassy-jelly, festive Canadian flavors, perfect for a quick snack or meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, flake the cod fish into small pieces.
Add the flour, smoked paprika, Pecorino Romano cheese, egg, onion, celery, and carrots to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Heat about 1 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 375°F.
Using a spoon, scoop small amounts of the fish mixture into the hot oil and flatten slightly with a spatula.
Fry the fritters for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the fritters on paper towels and serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
For an extra crispy coating, mix the breadcrumbs and hot sauce in a shallow dish and toss the fritters in the mixture to coat before serving.
